---
title: "LRPDID07: Waitlist-crossover observational period-resolved session-dose model for letter-sound knowledge (LS)"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

{{< include _partials/_header.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_setup.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_gate_badge.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_key_findings.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_reading_guide.qmd >}}

## Overview

LRPDID07 is the period-resolved observational session-dose model for letter-sound knowledge (YARC-LSK, `yarclet`). It retains the P1/P2 transition frame, randomised arm, current treatment, the pre-randomisation t1 letter-sound score and t1 age, then adds partially pooled period-specific slopes for treated-centred session attendance.

_The question this answers._ Does the residual dose association vary by period? The answer is not read off this fit alone: use the nested PSIS-LOO comparison with LRPDID07base in `did_dose_loo_compare.csv`, but interpret a ranking only when `comparison_valid` is true. If row identities differ or either fit fails its convergence gate, the file records why no ranking is valid.

_What this estimand is, and is not._ The per-period session count is the exposure (`IS`) and remains confounded by latent general ability and other determinants of attendance and learning. Arm, current treatment, t1 outcome and t1 age adjustment separates the randomised schedule and structural untreated zero from residual attendance variation; it cannot identify the causal effect of another session. Never write “more sessions cause more gain”. The causal letter-sound result remains the randomised t2 contrast in LRPITT07/LRPDID02.

_Sign convention and the ceiling._ Positive dose slopes mean higher-than-average attendance among treated rows is associated with a higher expected period-end letter-sound score. The Beta-Binomial logit respects the bounded 0–32 score; it does not make the association causal.

## Model

$$
\eta_{i,p} = \alpha + \beta_{\text{period}}\,[p = \text{P2}]
           + \beta_{\text{group}}G_i + \theta_{\text{treated}}\,\text{Treated}_{i,p}
           + \beta_{\text{dose},p}\,\text{dose\_treated\_std}_{i,p}
           + \gamma_{t1}\,\text{logit}_*(L_{t1,i}, N)
           + \gamma_A\,A_{t1,\text{std},i} + u_{\text{child},i},
$$

with $\beta_{\text{dose},p} = \mu_{\text{dose}} + \sigma_{\text{dose}}z_p$ (non-centred and partially pooled across periods), a Beta-Binomial likelihood on the period post-count, $N=32$, and $G_i=1$ for the immediate arm. `dose_treated_std` is centred and scaled among treated rows and set to zero for the untreated structural-zero cell; the t1 outcome is broadcast to both transition rows. Priors follow the shared template in `priors.py`.

![Model diagram](model_graph.png){#fig-model .lightbox fig-align="left"}

{{< include _partials/_priors.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_prior_predictive.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_results_did.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_technical.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_footer.qmd >}}
